Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint; ***Court Reporter Fees***
Off Calendar Jun-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. STEVEN R. RUTH, Esq. (Tel: 415-787-0880) for plaintiff David Gowdey;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DAVID GOWDEY's Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff David Bowdey's motion for leave to file a verified first amended complaint is granted. After default judgment was entered in plaintiff's favor, the Court of Appeal recently reversed the court's denial of defendants' motion to set aside the defaults and default judgments. Plaintiff now seeks to amend the complaint; the proposed amendment would substantially simplify and shorten the original pleading. The motion is supported by the strong policy favoring liberal amendment of pleadings. Defendants do not show that they would be prejudiced by the amendment. To the extent that defendants seek to attack the viability of the proposed amended complaint, they may do so by demurrer or other appropriate pretrial motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) Defendants are reminded that before filing a demurrer, they are required to meet and confer with plaintiff's counsel. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 430.41.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964 (302/EPS)

Application For Default Judgment By Court Against Defendants John Heseltine, Bridget Alison Mcneil And Cb Trustees 2013 Limited
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants John Heseltine, an individual, Bridget Alison McNeil, an individual and CB Trustees 2013 Limited, a New Zealand Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768. Email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

Steven R. Ruth, Esq. appeared for plaintiff David Gowdey, who was not present for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants John Heseltine, Bridget Alison McNeil or CB Trustees 2013 Limited, a New Zealand Company.
Plaintiff's complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on November 9, 2018.
Defaults as to defendants John Heseltine, Bridget Alison McNeil and CB Trustees 2013 Limited, a New Zealand Company, were entered on December 21, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on February 4, 2019.
Dismissal as to defendant Daniel Doulton was entered on May 28, 2019.
Order waiving witness appearance a default prove-up hearing was filed on July 19, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff David Gowdey, and against the defaulted defendants John Heseltine, an individual, Bridget Alison McNeil, an individual and CB Trustees 2013 Limited, a New Zealand Company, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $513,617.64.
Prejudgment Interest - $53,816.58.
Costs - $810.26.

Total - $568,244.48.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
